sections.heading: Why did we make these changes?
sections.body:
We redesigned the navigation to provide a more composable and scalable
structure so that our developer data platform can grow over time. We
redesigned the navigation to improve the self-discovery of the wide
array of services we offer and to provide clear navigation across
services and resources. Ultimately, these changes set a foundation to
support the growth of our products and services over time.

sections.heading: What's changing?
sections.body:
- We simplified the left sidebar navigation. We organized the sidebar
  navigation into the following categories:

  - **Database**

  - **Streaming Data**

  - **Services**

  - **Security**

  These categories provide distinct yet flexible containers that
  display the breadth of our platform services and a clear division
  between the main tasks.

- We clarified the resource context. The top navigation persists and
  indicates the current resource (organization, project,
  collection, and so on). You can easily switch between resources to
  improve workflow continuity.

- We unified utilities. A centralized utility hub in the top-right
  corner of the UI provides quick and easy access to essential tools
  like alerts, the activity feed, support, and user management. You can
  also find links to MongoDB University, Documentation, and
  Community Forums here.

- We removed the distinct product tabs. We now present all primary
  services in the appropriate category beneath one consolidated sidebar
  navigation, which makes these capabilities easier to find. We removed
  the top navigation tabs for **Data Services** and
  **Charts**. We changed the label for **Charts** to
  **Visualizations**.

sections.heading: Where can I find the list of projects in my organization?
sections.body:
At the organization level, you can now click **Organization Overview** to view a list of
projects in an organization.

sections.heading: Where can I find federated authentication settings?
sections.body:
At the organization level, you can now click **Federation** in
the sidebar under the **Identity & Access** heading.

sections.heading: Where can I find billing information?
sections.body:
At the organization level, you can now click **Overview**,
**Invoices**, **Cost Explorer**, or
**Linked Organizations** in the sidebar under the
**Billing** heading.

sections.heading: Where can I find my integrations?
sections.body:
At the organization level, you can now click the
**Integrations** tab in the project or organization settings.

sections.heading: Where can I find my cluster?
sections.body:
At the project level, you can now click **Clusters** in the
sidebar under the **Database** header.

sections.heading: Where can I find my backups?
sections.body:
At the project level, you can now click **Backup** in the
sidebar under the **Database** header.

sections.heading: Where can I find Charts?
sections.body:
At the project level, you can now click **Visualization** in
the sidebar under the **Services** header.

sections.heading: Where can I find MongoDB Search and MongoDB Vector Search?
sections.body:
At the project level, you can now click
**Search & Vector Search** in the sidebar under the
**Database** header.

sections.heading: Where can I find triggers and functions?
sections.body:
At the project level, you can now click **Triggers** in the
sidebar under the **Streaming Data** header.

sections.heading: Where can I find the Data API and Device Sync?
sections.body:
The Data API and Device Sync only appear if you have them enabled for
you project. At the project level, you can now click
**Data API**  or **Device Sync** in the sidebar under
the **Services** header.

The Data API page also includes HTTPS Endpoints and GraphQL. GraphQL
will be deprecated.

sections.heading: Where can I find project identity and access settings?
sections.body:
At the project level, you can now click
**Project Identity & Access** in the sidebar under the
**Security** header.

sections.heading: Where can I find the advanced settings?
sections.body:
At the project level you can now click
**Database & Network Access** under the **Security**
heading and then click **Advanced** in the sidebar.

sections.heading: What pages were removed?
sections.body:
The **Overview** page for your account, and the
**Project Support Page** were removed.

sections.heading: What links were removed?
sections.body:
The **New on Atlas** and **Goto** links were removed
from the sidebar.

The **Dev Center** link was removed from the
**All Products** menu.

sections.heading: When did the navigation changes launch?
sections.body:
We rolled out the new navigation by organizations (for all users
in an organization simultaneously) on October 13, 2025.

sections.heading: Can I turn off the new navigation experience?
sections.body: No, you cannot toggle off the new navigation experience.

sections.heading: What will I see when I first log in?
sections.body:
You will see a modal that announces the change. After you click
**See what's new**, a short guided tour shows the key changes.
You can dismiss the tour at any time.

sections.heading: Where can I learn more?
sections.body:
There will be a series of announcements across our social channels and
blogs.

sections.heading: If I have multiple organizations, will they change at the same time?
sections.body:
No, we cannot guarantee that organizations under the same account will
receive the navigation changes simultaneously.

sections.heading: Is the documentation updated to reflect these changes?
sections.body:
Documentation updates that align with the new navigation are now
live to everyone. We updated all pages to reflect the
new navigation.

sections.heading: What products are impacted?
sections.body:
Atlas, [Atlas for Government](atlas/government), Charts,
and Cloud Manager are impacted.
